articleSummary: The articleis a practical analysis aimed at developers and system‑administrators who need fast, cost‑effective NVMe storage in the cloud. Its core thesis is that, among the major providers surveyed in mid‑2017, Amazon’s EC2 i3 family (especially i3.large and larger) delivers the best price‑to‑I/O ratio and overall performance, with ultra‑fast NVMe SSDs (up to 1 GB/s) at competitive monthly rates. The author backs this claim with head‑to‑head benchmark tables and price comparisons, noting that network bandwidth—often advertised as “up to 10 Gb/s”—is the primary bottleneck in real‑world tests. The tone is a concise, data‑driven tutorial, using straightforward “TLDR” summaries and visual charts rather than rhetorical flourishes. The piece is framed as a quick‑reference guide for choosing cloud instances with NVMe storage, with a focus on AWS but also listing other providers for context.

The article "Compare NVMe SSD Cloud Options" analyzes AWS EC2 instances with NVMe SSD storage as the most cost-effective and high-performance solution for cloud workloads, despite limitations in network throughput. It highlights AWS i3.large/i3.2xlarge instances (475GB–1.9TB NVMe SSDs) as benchmarks for price/performance efficiency, contrasting them with providers like Digital Ocean and Packet.net. The tone is analytical, blending technical data (pricing, I/O benchmarks) with practical warnings about overhyped network speeds. Framed as a 2018-era guide, it targets cloud architects and developers seeking optimized storage solutions, using AWS as a case study to stress-test NVMe SSD value propositions. Key framing devices include "TLDR;"
